channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Inflatable tiny house with space for six is blowing up
item.description: An inflatable six-person tiny house from a firm called 2001 looks like a space station with its inflatable dome that is transportable and eco-friendly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This $4299 laptop comes with 128GB RAM by default plus an AMD Ryzen AI 9 APU, and I can't wait to test it
item.description: HP's $4299 EliteBook X G1a laptop has 128GB RAM and challenges the most expensive 14-inch MacBook Pro.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: We're getting achingly closer to SOT-MRAM; the memory that could one day replace DRAM and NAND, but I can't see it happening soon
item.description: Researchers claim to have reached a goal that will allow SOT-MRAM to become reality sooner, just not too soon.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Hop in, no driver needed for this future ride-hailing robotaxi
item.description: Zeekr and Waymo are joining forces for the world's first mass-produced autonomous vehicle, combining an electric vehicle with self-driving technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NASCAR 25, the new game from iRacing, will put Daytona 500 fans in the driver’s seat
item.description: The Daytona 500 is one of the more challenging races on the NASCAR circuit. The speedway is long and narrow, forcing drivers to be more aggressive. And the weather in central Florida doesn’t always cooperate. During the 2024 event, a deluge of rain had forced a Monday conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Callahan, Fast Company

item.title: Everything you need to know about Elon Musk’s DOGE staffers
item.description: Though only a few weeks old, already the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) is radically changing the face of bureaucracy in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Lily McDonald, Fast Company

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 14 states sue Musk and Trump, calling billionaire's role unconstitutional
item.description: Fourteen states sued Elon Musk, his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) and President Trump on Thursday, arguing that the tech billionaire’s sweeping efforts to cut government spending are unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck owner says he’s been threatened and harassed soon after Musk endorsed Trump
item.description: Dr. Kumait Jaroje said someone put a profane sticker on his gold Cybertruck. When he posted a photo of it on social media he told police he started getting threats online.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: The rebirth of Pebble is radically unambitious
item.description: Eric Migicovsky has barely started working on a successor to the Pebble smartwatch, and he’s already talking about being finished with it. Eight years ago, Migicovsky shut down the smartwatch startup he founded, having sold its software assets to Fitbit, which later became part of Google.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps
item.description: Creating a forward-thinking framework that keeps sight of what came before makes disruption and digital maturity possible, says Leidos CIO Alexandra Guenther. The post How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Screenshot-scanning malware discovered on Apple App Store in first-of-its-kind attack
item.description: Security experts have discovered that hackers are targeting apps on the App Store to spread malware that steals information from saved screenshots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI chatbots outperform doctors in diagnosing patients, study finds
item.description: Chatbots quickly surpassed human physicians in diagnostic reasoning — the crucial first step in clinical care — according to a new study published in the journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HPE expands ProLiant server portfolio, boosts AI and security features
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) added eight new servers to its ProLiant Gen 12 server portfolio, bringing advanced security and AI-optimization features to enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Snowflake announces preview of Cortex Agent APIs to power enterprise data intelligence
item.description: Snowflake on Wednesday announced the public preview of Cortex Agents, a set of APIs built on top of the Snowflake Intelligence platform, a low-code offering that was first launched in November at Build, the company’s annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: FPGAs lose luster in genAI era
item.description: In a world gone mad over GPUs for AI acceleration, FPGA processors are seeing limited adoption in the data center for genAI workloads, with their primary use still in embedded markets and edge AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Internet upgrade part of a move towards 400/800G connectivity
item.description: The industry transition to 400 Gigabit Ethernet networking took a big step forward this week when the world’s leading Internet exchange operator announced plans to upgrade its New York backbone to 400G.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4
